content: ICM's intrinsic curiosity-driven exploration can be regulated by an autonomous agent's working memory capacity limit: agents will automatically assign higher intrinsic reward to unexplored state features that fit within their remaining working memory, and deprioritize novel states that would overcrowd their active buffer. This creates a naturally self-limiting curiosity that avoids the common ICM problem of over-exploring noisy, unprocessable novel states.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
